mechanism: A sudden widening of cross-sectional crypto returns is treated as a fragmentation and stress state. Assets that lose when dispersion widens provide poor insurance and must offer a return premium; assets that rise in those states are hedges that investors may accept at lower expected returns.
payer: Investors paying for protection against broad fragmentation and participants forced to reduce assets that perform poorly when market dispersion expands.

data.source: existing local closed OKX perpetual data strictly resampled to closed 1-hour bars

frozen_rule.primary_dispersion: cross-sectional standard deviation of hourly log returns
frozen_rule.characteristic: negative rolling beta of each asset return to the dispersion shock
frozen_rule.weighting: equal 50 percent long and 50 percent short

anti_rescue: do not reverse the direction after seeing results
do not redefine dispersion after seeing results
do not exclude BTC or any other eligible symbol
do not change the 42-day lookback or weekly schedule
do not add momentum, volatility, liquidity, funding or sector filters
do not open future returns or PnL unless every pre-PnL gate passes
